name: Uniworld
description: Uniworld Boutique River Cruises is a luxury river cruise line offering all-inclusive itineraries across Europe, Egypt, India, Peru, and Southeast Asia.

name: Avalon Waterways
description: Avalon Waterways is a river cruise company offering voyages across Europe, Asia, South America, and the Galápagos Islands, known for its spacious Suite Ships and personalized service.

name: Aurora Expeditions
description: Aurora Expeditions is an Australian-owned company specialising in small-ship expedition cruises to remote destinations across all seven continents, with a focus on polar regions.

name: AMA Waterways
description: AmaWaterways is a luxury river cruise company offering all-inclusive voyages across Europe, Asia, Africa, and South America, known for spacious ships, gourmet dining, and cultural immersion.

name: Atlas Cruises
description: Atlas Ocean Voyages is a U.S.-based luxury expedition cruise line established in September 2019 as a subsidiary of Portugal's Mystic Invest Holding. Headquartered in Fort Lauderdale, Florida, the company operates small, purpose-built ships designed for polar-class voyages, offering all-inclusive experiences to destinations such as Antarctica, the Arctic, and the Mediterranean. Their fleet includes the World Navigator (2021), World Traveller (2022), and World Voyager (2023), each accommodating up to 200 guests. A fourth ship, World Seeker, is scheduled to debut in 2025. Atlas Ocean Voyages emphasizes intimate yachting expeditions, combining adventure with luxury amenities.

name: Australis
description: Expedition cruises through the remote fjords, channels, and glaciers of Patagonia and Tierra del Fuego with expert naturalist guides.

name: American Queen Voyages
description: Authentic paddlewheel riverboat cruises on America's great rivers, offering historical and cultural exploration with traditional riverboat charm.

name: Azamara
description: Azamara is a luxury cruise line specializing in Destination Immersion® experiences, offering longer stays, more overnights, and access to smaller ports inaccessible to larger ships. Founded in 2007, Azamara operates four boutique ships: Azamara Journey®, Azamara Quest®, Azamara Pursuit®, and Azamara Onward®, each accommodating up to 702 guests. The company is headquartered in Miami, Florida, and is a subsidiary of Sycamore Partners, a private equity firm specializing in consumer, retail, and distribution investments.

name: AIDA Cruises
description: Germany's most popular cruise brand featuring casual club-ship atmosphere with active entertainment, family-friendly facilities, and distinctive colorful ship designs.

name: Princess Cruises
description: Princess Cruises is an American cruise line headquartered in Santa Clarita, California, and is a subsidiary of Carnival Corporation & plc. Founded in 1965, the company operates a fleet of 15 ships offering global itineraries to destinations such as Alaska, the Caribbean, Europe, and Asia. Princess Cruises is renowned for its innovative ships, diverse onboard options, and exceptional customer service. The company is also recognized for its environmental initiatives, including the introduction of the Sun Princess, the first ship in its fleet powered by liquefied natural gas (LNG), significantly reducing emissions. Additionally, the Sun Princess features technology that converts over 95% of its combustible waste into reusable thermal energy, making it one of the most eco-friendly cruise ships in the world. For more information, visit their official website at https://www.princess.com/.

name: Hurtigruten
description: Hurtigruten is a Norwegian cruise line founded in 1893, renowned for its coastal voyages along Norway's coastline. The company operates daily, year-round services connecting 34 ports between Bergen and Kirkenes, offering both passenger and cargo transport. Hurtigruten also provides expedition cruises to destinations like the Arctic and Antarctica, emphasizing sustainability and responsible tourism. Their fleet includes hybrid-powered ships, and they are committed to building a fully zero-emission vessel by 2030.

name: Silversea Cruises
description: Silversea Cruises is a luxury cruise line headquartered in Monaco, renowned for its all-inclusive voyages and global destinations. Founded in 1994, it became part of the Royal Caribbean Group in July 2018, with the remaining shares acquired in July 2020. Silversea's fleet includes both ocean-going and expedition ships, offering immersive journeys to all seven continents.

name: Disney Cruise Line
description: Disney Cruise Line is a subsidiary of The Walt Disney Company, offering themed cruise vacations to various destinations worldwide. Established in 1996, the line operates a fleet of ships, including Disney Magic, Disney Wonder, Disney Dream, Disney Fantasy, Disney Wish, and Disney Treasure. Upcoming additions include Disney Destiny in November 2025 and Disney Adventure in March 2026. The line also owns private islands in the Bahamas, Castaway Cay and Lookout Cay at Lighthouse Point, providing exclusive ports of call for its ships.

name: Seabourn Cruise Line
description: Seabourn Cruise Line is an American ultra-luxury cruise line headquartered in Seattle, Washington. Founded in 1986, it operates a fleet of intimate ships offering all-suite accommodations, personalized service, and gourmet dining experiences. Seabourn is a subsidiary of Carnival Corporation & plc and is a proud member of the World's Leading Cruise Lines alliance, which includes brands like Holland America Line, Princess Cruises, and Cunard Line. The cruise line offers voyages to destinations worldwide, including the Caribbean, Mediterranean, and Arctic regions, with itineraries ranging from seven to over 100 days. Seabourn's ships are known for their yacht-like atmosphere, providing access to exclusive ports beyond traditional cruise routes. All suites feature ocean views, and the crew-to-guest ratio is close to 1:1, ensuring exceptional service. Dining is all-inclusive, featuring restaurants by Michelin-starred chefs and unique experiences like 'Caviar in the Surf.' Seabourn's expedition ships allow guests to explore remote regions using Zodiacs, submarines, and onboard expert teams. The onboard atmosphere is both sophisticated and relaxed, like a private club enriched by cultural events, shared moments, and constant attention to every guest. In 2024, Seabourn earned over sixty prestigious awards, including recognition as the 'Best Small Ship' and 'Best Expedition Ship' by readers of Condé Nast Traveler. The Seabourn Ovation and Seabourn Pursuit were featured in Condé Nast Traveler’s 'Gold List' and 'Hot List,' respectively, recognizing the world’s best ships and standout new offerings. In 2024, the company took second place in U.S. News rankings for luxury cruises, couples’ cruises, and Mediterranean cruises. Travel Weekly named it Best Small Ship Cruise Line (under 1,000 passengers), while the Magellan Awards honored it across categories including service, cabins, dining, entertainment, and polar exploration, cementing Seabourn’s position as the benchmark for ultra-luxury at sea.

name: Oceania Cruises
description: Oceania Cruises is a luxury cruise line headquartered in Miami, Florida, offering destination-focused itineraries aboard small, elegant ships that accommodate up to 1,250 guests. Renowned for its "Finest Cuisine at Sea," the line provides voyages to over 600 ports across more than 100 countries, with itineraries ranging from 7 to over 200 days. As a subsidiary of Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd., Oceania Cruises is part of a diversified cruise operator that includes Norwegian Cruise Line and Regent Seven Seas Cruises.

name: Ponant
description: French-flagged luxury expedition line with a fleet of intimate, stylish yachts.

name: Windstar Cruises
description: Windstar Cruises is a U.S.-based cruise line specializing in small-ship luxury voyages. Founded in 1984, it operates a fleet of six yachts, each accommodating between 148 and 342 guests. The fleet includes both sailing yachts and all-suite motor yachts, offering itineraries to over 330 ports across Europe, the Caribbean, Costa Rica, the Panama Canal, Asia, Alaska, Canada, New Zealand, the South Pacific, Central America, Mexico, the U.S. West Coast, and year-round cruises in Tahiti. Windstar is recognized for its immersive experiences, exceptional service, and culinary excellence, being the official cruise line of the James Beard Foundation. The company is owned by Xanterra Travel Collection, a subsidiary of The Anschutz Corporation.

name: Cunard Line
description: Cunard Line is a British cruise line with a rich history spanning over 180 years, offering luxury ocean voyages aboard its fleet of four ships: Queen Mary 2, Queen Victoria, Queen Elizabeth, and the newest addition, Queen Anne. The company is headquartered at Carnival House in Southampton, England, and is a subsidiary of Carnival Corporation & plc. Cunard operates cruises to destinations including the Transatlantic, Mediterranean, Northern Europe, Caribbean, and World Cruises. In 2024, Cunard reported record-breaking bookings, with a 23% year-on-year increase compared to 2023, attributed to the launch of Queen Anne and new cruise programs.

name: Regent Seven Seas Cruises
description: Regent Seven Seas Cruises is a luxury cruise line headquartered in Miami, Florida, offering all-inclusive voyages to over 450 destinations worldwide. Their fleet includes ships like Seven Seas Explorer, Seven Seas Mariner, Seven Seas Voyager, Seven Seas Splendor, and Seven Seas Navigator, each providing spacious all-suite accommodations with private balconies. The line is known for its personalized service, fine dining, unlimited shore excursions, and comprehensive inclusions such as gratuities, Wi-Fi, and pre-cruise hotel packages. In October 2024, Regent announced the construction of a new ship, Seven Seas Prestige, scheduled for delivery in 2026, which will feature the largest suite at sea, the Skyview Regent Suite, spanning 9,000 square feet and accommodating up to six guests.

name: Virgin Voyages
description: Virgin Voyages is a cruise line headquartered in Plantation, Florida, operating as a joint venture between the Virgin Group and Bain Capital. Established in December 2014, the company aims to redefine the cruising experience with a focus on adult-only voyages, innovative amenities, and a commitment to sustainability. As of September 2025, Virgin Voyages' fleet comprises four ships, each accommodating approximately 2,700 passengers. The inaugural ship, Scarlet Lady, began operations in August 2021, followed by Valiant Lady in 2021, Resilient Lady in May 2023, and Brilliant Lady in September 2025. The company has secured significant funding, including a $550 million capital raise in September 2023, to support its expansion and growth plans.

name: Viking Ocean Cruises
description: Viking Ocean Cruises, a division of Viking Cruises, offers destination-focused ocean voyages on a fleet of modern, state-of-the-art ships. Founded in 2013, the company has grown to operate 92 vessels, providing itineraries that emphasize cultural immersion and extended time in port. In 2024, Viking Holdings went public on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol 'VIK', achieving a valuation of $11 billion.

name: Scenic Luxury Cruises & Tours
description: Scenic Luxury Cruises & Tours, established in 1986 by Australian businessman Glen Moroney, offers ultra-luxury river and ocean cruises, as well as guided land tours. The company operates 15 river cruise ships in Europe and Southeast Asia, and two ocean-going vessels, Scenic Eclipse and Scenic Eclipse II, which explore destinations worldwide, including the Americas, Europe, the Arctic, Antarctica, and the Norwegian fjords. Scenic is renowned for its all-inclusive luxury experience, covering all meals, beverages, Wi-Fi, butler service, shore excursions, and gratuities. Their fleet features state-of-the-art ships, such as the Scenic Space-Ships in Europe and the Scenic Eclipse Discovery Yachts, equipped with amenities like helicopters and submarines for unparalleled exploration.

name: Paul Gauguin Cruises
description: Paul Gauguin Cruises is a luxury cruise line specializing in voyages to Tahiti, French Polynesia, and the South Pacific. Operating the m/s Paul Gauguin, the line offers all-inclusive experiences with spacious oceanview accommodations, gourmet dining, and exclusive beach retreats. Founded in 1998, it became a subsidiary of Compagnie du Ponant in 2019.

name: Emerald Cruises
description: Emerald Cruises is a luxury cruise line offering both river and yacht cruises. Founded in 2013 as Emerald Waterways, it became Emerald Cruises in 2020, unifying its river and yacht cruise operations. The company is part of the Scenic Group and is headquartered in Zug, Switzerland. Emerald Cruises operates a fleet of river ships and luxury yachts, providing all-inclusive experiences with amenities such as gourmet dining, complimentary beverages, gratuities, Wi-Fi, and daily shore excursions. Their itineraries cover destinations in Europe, Southeast Asia, the Mediterranean, the Caribbean, and the Red Sea.

name: Marella Cruises
description: British all-inclusive cruise line, part of the TUI Group.

name: American Cruise Lines
description: Small ship cruising on U.S. rivers and coastlines.

name: SeaDream Yacht Club
description: SeaDream Yacht Club is a privately owned cruise line headquartered in Oslo, Norway, founded in 2001 by Atle Brynestad. The company operates two intimate mega-yachts, SeaDream I and SeaDream II, each accommodating up to 112 guests and 95 crew members. These yachts offer luxury cruises to destinations including the Caribbean, Mediterranean, Northern Europe, and Asia-Pacific. SeaDream emphasizes a personalized, casual luxury experience, with all ocean-view staterooms, inclusive open bar, gratuities, and watersports marina. The company is known for its award-winning service and world-class cuisine.

name: Hapag-Lloyd Cruises
description: Luxury and expedition cruises with bilingual (German/English) service.

name: Ritz-Carlton Yacht Collection
description: The Ritz-Carlton Yacht Collection offers luxury voyages aboard custom-built yachts, combining the renowned Ritz-Carlton hospitality with the freedom of yachting. Their fleet includes the Evrima, Ilma, and Luminara, each featuring spacious suites with private terraces, multiple dining venues, and personalized service. These yachts sail to destinations such as the Caribbean, Mediterranean, and Asia-Pacific regions, providing an intimate and all-inclusive cruising experience.

name: Lindblad Expeditions
description: Expedition cruising in partnership with National Geographic.

name: Hansa Touristik
description: German cruise operator offering traditional ocean cruises.

name: Explora Journeys
description: Explora Journeys is a luxury cruise line established in 2021 as a division of the Mediterranean Shipping Company (MSC). The brand focuses on redefining ocean travel through elegance and exclusivity, offering unique and immersive experiences. Their fleet includes EXPLORA I, launched in August 2023, and EXPLORA II, which began service in September 2024. Future ships, EXPLORA III and EXPLORA IV, are scheduled for 2026 and 2027, respectively, with plans to incorporate LNG and hydrogen fuel technologies to enhance sustainability. Explora Journeys' offerings include spacious ocean-front suites, diverse culinary experiences, and a commitment to environmental sustainability, aiming for net-zero carbon emissions by 2050.

name: Fred Olsen Cruise Lines
description: Traditional British cruising on smaller, classic ships.

name: Celestyal Cruises
description: Specializing in Greek Islands and Eastern Mediterranean itineraries.

name: Crystal Cruises
description: Crystal Cruises is a luxury American cruise line founded in 1988. After a period of insolvency in 2022, the brand was acquired by A&K Travel Group, which relaunched operations in 2023. The fleet includes the Crystal Serenity and Crystal Symphony, both of which underwent extensive refurbishments prior to their return to service. Crystal Cruises offers ultra-luxury voyages with a focus on personalized service, gourmet dining, and exclusive shore excursions.

name: Costa Cruises
description: Italian cruise line offering Mediterranean flair and family-friendly cruises

name: Carnival Cruise Line
description: Carnival Cruise Line is a leading international cruise operator headquartered in Doral, Florida. As a subsidiary of Carnival Corporation & plc, it is renowned for offering a wide range of cruise vacations from various U.S. homeports. The company is recognized for its distinctive red, white, and blue funnel logo, symbolizing its commitment to fun and adventure at sea.

name: Norwegian Cruise Line
description: Norwegian Cruise Line (NCL) is an American cruise line founded in 1966, known for pioneering the 'Freestyle Cruising' concept, which offers passengers flexible dining times and relaxed dress codes. Headquartered in Miami, Florida, NCL operates a fleet of 20 contemporary ships that sail to nearly 400 destinations across seven continents. The company is a subsidiary of Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd., which also owns Oceania Cruises and Regent Seven Seas Cruises. In 2024, NCL announced plans to order eight new ships, marking the largest ship order in its history, with deliveries scheduled between 2026 and 2036. As of September 26, 2025, Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. (NCLH) is publicly traded on the New York Stock Exchange, with a stock price of $25.04 USD.

name: MSC Cruises
description: MSC Cruises is the world's third-largest cruise line, headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland. As part of the privately held MSC Group, it operates a modern fleet of 23 ships, offering cruises across five continents and visiting over 100 countries. The company employs more than 30,000 staff globally and is a market leader in Europe, South America, the Middle East, and Southern Africa, with a growing presence in North America and the Far East.

name: Holland America Line
description: Holland America Line is an American cruise line operating as a subsidiary of Carnival Corporation & plc. Founded in 1873 in Rotterdam, Netherlands, it offers cruises to nearly 400 ports in 114 countries, including destinations like Alaska, the Caribbean, Europe, and Asia. The fleet comprises 11 ships, each carrying between 1,432 and 2,668 guests, providing a moderately upscale cruise experience. In 2024, the line became the first global cruise line to receive seafood certifications from the Marine Stewardship Council and the Aquaculture Stewardship Council, ensuring sustainably and responsibly caught seafood on board. Additionally, a new 'Dutch Day' was introduced on every itinerary, honoring the line's heritage with themed dinners, parties, and Dutch treats like poffertjes and stroopwafels. The company is headquartered in Seattle, Washington, with Beth Bodensteiner serving as President.

name: Celebrity Cruises
description: Celebrity Cruises is a premium cruise line headquartered in Miami, Florida, and a wholly owned subsidiary of Royal Caribbean Group. Founded in 1988 by the Greece-based Chandris Group, Celebrity Cruises offers upscale vacation experiences across a fleet of ships traveling to nearly 300 destinations in over 70 countries. The company is known for its commitment to luxury, superior design, spacious accommodations, attentive service, and exceptional cuisine. Celebrity Cruises is set to debut its newest ship, Celebrity Xcel, in Fall 2025.

name: Royal Caribbean International
description: Royal Caribbean International is a Norwegian–American cruise line founded in 1968, operating as a subsidiary of Royal Caribbean Group since 1997. Headquartered in Miami, Florida, it is the largest cruise line by revenue and the second largest by passenger count, controlling 27.0% of the worldwide cruise market by passengers and 24.8% by revenue as of 2025. The company operates 29 ships as of August 2025, with three additional ships on order and two planned without launch dates. Royal Caribbean International also operates two private resorts: Labadee in Haiti and CocoCay in the Bahamas. The company is planning to establish the Royal Beach Club on Paradise Island in Nassau, Bahamas, in December 2025.
